title: Agents

config.title: Adapter Configuration
config.modelHint: Select a model for this agent
config.modelRequired: Pi requires a configured model in provider/model format.
config.noModelDetected: No model detected. Select or enter one manually.
config.instructionsFileHint: Absolute path to a markdown file (e.g. AGENTS.md) that defines this agent's behavior. Injected into the system prompt at runtime.
config.bootstrapPromptHint: Optional initial setup prompt for the first run

environmentCheck.title: Adapter environment check
environmentCheck.description: Runs a live probe that asks the adapter CLI to respond with hello.

onboarding.firstTaskPlaceholder: e.g. Review the codebase and create a project plan

onboarding.firstTask: Hire your first engineer and create a hiring plan
onboarding.selectCompanyFirst: Create or select a company before testing adapter environment.
onboarding.opencodeModelRequired: OpenCode requires an explicit model in provider/model format.
onboarding.retryFailed: Retried with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset in adapter config, but the environment test is still failing.
onboarding.retryUnsetFailed: Failed to unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and retry.

adapters.title: Adapters
adapters.installDescription: Add an adapter from npm or a local path. The adapter package must export createServerAdapter().
adapters.pathHint: Accepts Linux, WSL, and Windows paths. Windows paths are auto-converted.
adapters.alphaDescription: The adapter plugin system is under active development. APIs and storage format may change. Use the power icon to hide adapters from agent menus without removing them.
adapters.noExternalAdaptersHint: Install an adapter package to extend model support.
adapters.reinstallDescription: This will pull the latest version of {{package}} from npm and hot-swap the running adapter module. Existing agents will use the new version on their next run.
adapters.removeDescription: Are you sure you want to remove the {{type}} adapter? It will be unregistered and removed from the adapter store.{{npmCleanup}} This action cannot be undone.
adapters.npmCleanup: npm packages will be cleaned up from disk.

detail.failedToLoadWorkspaceLog: Failed to load workspace operation log
detail.pendingApproval: This agent is pending board approval and cannot be invoked yet.
detail.canCreateAgentsHint: Lets this agent create or hire agents and implicitly assign tasks.
detail.taskAssignAgentCreator: Enabled automatically while this agent can create new agents.
detail.taskAssignExplicitGrant: Enabled via explicit company permission grant.
detail.instructionsBundleOnlyLocal: Instructions bundles are only available for local adapters.
detail.modeManagedTooltip: Managed: Paperclip stores and serves the instructions bundle. External: you provide a path on disk where the instructions live.
detail.rootPathTooltip: The absolute directory on disk where the instructions bundle lives. In managed mode this is set by Paperclip automatically.
detail.entryFileTooltip: The main file the agent reads first when loading instructions. Defaults to AGENTS.md.
detail.virtualFileTooltip: Legacy inline prompt — this deprecated virtual file preserves the old promptTemplate content
detail.importSkillsFirst: Import skills into the company library first, then attach them here.
detail.unmanagedSkills: ({{count}}) User-installed skills, not managed by Paperclip
detail.requestedSkillsMissing: Requested skills missing from the company library
detail.manageSkillsDirectly: Manage skills in the adapter directly.
detail.unsupportedCustomAcp: Paperclip cannot manage skills for custom ACP commands yet.
detail.unsupportedOpenClaw: Paperclip cannot manage OpenClaw skills here. Visit your OpenClaw instance to manage this agent's skills.
detail.unsupportedAdapter: Paperclip cannot manage skills for this adapter yet. Manage them in the adapter directly.
detail.clearSessionForIssues: Clear session for {{count}} issue{{plural}} touched by this run?
detail.noPersistedTranscript: No persisted transcript for this run.
detail.showingFirstOf: Showing the first {{kb}} KB of {{total}} KB
detail.apiKeyCreated: API key created — copy it now, it will not be shown again.
detail.apiKeyDescription: API keys allow this agent to authenticate calls to the Paperclip server.

configForm.name: Name
configForm.title: Title
configForm.capabilitiesPlaceholder: Describe what this agent can do...
configForm.promptTemplatePlaceholder: You are agent {{ agent.name }}. Your role is {{ agent.role }}...
configForm.promptTemplateWarning: Prompt template is replayed on every heartbeat. Keep it compact and dynamic to avoid recurring token cost and cache churn.
configForm.defaultEnvironmentHint: Agent-level default execution target. Project and issue settings can still override this.
configForm.noModelDetected: No model detected. Select or enter one manually.
configForm.opencodeNonLocalDiscovery: Live OpenCode model discovery only runs for Local environments. Using the curated list and manual entry for {{name}}.
configForm.codexMinimalSearchWarning: Codex may reject `minimal` thinking when search is enabled.
configForm.bootstrapPromptPlaceholder: Optional initial setup prompt for the first run
configForm.bootstrapPromptWarning: Bootstrap prompt is legacy and will be removed in a future release. Consider moving this content into the agent's prompt template or instructions file instead.
configForm.noModelDetectedYet: No model detected yet. Enter a provider/model manually.
configForm.cheapModelHint: Used when a run requests the cheap profile (e.g. routine summaries). The primary model stays unchanged.
configForm.cheapModelNoDefault: No adapter default — choose a cheaper model
configForm.cheapModelFallback: No explicit cheap model selected — runtime falls back to {{model}}.
configForm.cheapModelNoSelection: No cheap model selected and the adapter has no default. Cheap-lane runs will continue on the primary model with a fallback note.

help.name: Display name for this agent.
help.title: Job title shown in the org chart.
help.role: Organizational role. Determines position and capabilities.
help.reportsTo: The agent this one reports to in the org hierarchy.
help.capabilities: Describes what this agent can do. Shown in the org chart and used for task routing.
help.adapterType: How this agent runs: local CLI (Claude/Codex/OpenCode), OpenClaw Gateway, spawned process, or generic HTTP webhook.
help.cwd: Deprecated legacy working directory fallback for local adapters. Existing agents may still carry this value, but new configurations should use project workspaces instead.
help.promptTemplate: Sent on every heartbeat. Keep this small and dynamic. Use it for current-task framing, not large static instructions. Supports {{ agent.id }}, {{ agent.name }}, {{ agent.role }} and other template variables.
help.model: Override the default model used by the adapter.
help.thinkingEffort: Control model reasoning depth. Supported values vary by adapter/model.
help.chrome: Enable Claude's Chrome integration by passing --chrome.
help.dangerouslySkipPermissions: Run unattended by auto-approving adapter permission prompts when supported.
help.dangerouslyBypassSandbox: Run Codex without sandbox restrictions. Required for filesystem/network access.
help.search: Enable Codex web search capability during runs.
help.fastMode: Enable Codex Fast mode. This burns credits/tokens much faster and is supported on GPT-5.4 and manual Codex model IDs.
help.workspaceStrategy: How Paperclip should realize an execution workspace for this agent. Keep project_primary for normal cwd execution, or use git_worktree for issue-scoped isolated checkouts.
help.workspaceBaseRef: Base git ref used when creating a worktree branch. Leave blank to use the resolved workspace ref or HEAD.
help.workspaceBranchTemplate: Template for naming derived branches. Supports {{issue.identifier}}, {{issue.title}}, {{agent.name}}, {{project.id}}, {{workspace.repoRef}}, and {{slug}}.
help.worktreeParentDir: Directory where derived worktrees should be created. Absolute, ~-prefixed, and repo-relative paths are supported.
help.runtimeServicesJson: Optional workspace runtime service definitions. Use this for shared app servers, workers, or other long-lived companion processes attached to the workspace.
help.maxTurnsPerRun: Maximum number of agentic turns (tool calls) per heartbeat run.
help.command: The command to execute (e.g. node, python).
help.localCommand: Override the path to the CLI command you want the adapter to call (e.g. /usr/local/bin/claude, codex, opencode).
help.extraArgs: Extra CLI arguments for local adapters, comma-separated.
help.envVars: Environment variables injected into the adapter process. Use plain values or secret references.
help.bootstrapPrompt: Only sent when Paperclip starts a fresh session. Use this for stable setup guidance that should not be repeated on every heartbeat.
help.payloadTemplateJson: Optional JSON merged into remote adapter request payloads before Paperclip adds its standard wake and workspace fields.
help.webhookUrl: The URL that receives POST requests when the agent is invoked.
help.heartbeatInterval: Run this agent automatically on a timer. Useful for periodic tasks like checking for new work.
help.timeoutSec: Maximum seconds a run can take before being terminated. 0 means no timeout.
help.graceSec: Seconds to wait after sending interrupt before force-killing the process.
help.wakeOnDemand: Allow this agent to be woken by assignments, API calls, UI actions, or automated systems.
help.cooldownSec: Minimum seconds between consecutive heartbeat runs.
help.maxConcurrentRuns: Maximum number of heartbeat runs that can execute simultaneously for this agent.
help.maxTurnContinuationEnabled: Automatically queue bounded continuation runs when an adapter stops because its per-run turn cap was exhausted.
help.maxTurnContinuationMaxAttempts: Maximum automatic continuations after one max-turn stop. This is separate from max turns per run.
help.maxTurnContinuationDelaySec: Seconds to wait before starting each max-turn continuation.
help.budgetMonthlyCents: Monthly spending limit in cents. 0 means no limit.
